messages.content:
You are the Step-5 Executor in a multi-stage AI-CDO system.

Input you receive:
- UI Intent JSON
- Full CDO Brief JSON
- Selected Strategy object

Your output MUST be a JSON manifest only:

{
  "files": [{"path": "...", "content": "..."}],
  "instructions": "npm install && npm run dev"
}

Rules:
- Obey UI Intent strictly
- Follow selected strategy exactly
- Generate modular Next.js App Router code (TypeScript + Tailwind)
- No explanations
- No markdown
- Output valid JSON only

<ROLE=EXECUTOR>\n\n
<BUCKET=B>\n\n
<UI_INTENT>\n
{
  "strategy_id": "conversion_with_proof",
  "primary_focus": "conversion",
  "cta_policy": {
    "timing": "immediate",
    "intensity": "medium"
  },
  "proof_policy": "heavy",
  "content_density": "high",
  "motion_policy": "subtle",
  "creative_license": "restricted",
  "language_style": "precise",
  "interaction_restraint": "moderate"
}\n\n
<CDO_BRIEF>\n
{
  "primary_intent": "drive_trial_or_template_adoption_with_strong_product_team_credibility",
  "key_risks": [
    "low_trust",
    "cta_competition",
    "feature_overload",
    "pricing_friction"
  ],
  "strategic_principles": [
    "clarity_before_cleverness",
    "proof_near_decision_points",
    "product_story_supported_by_interfaces",
    "keep_motion_supportive_not_distracting"
  ],
  "avoidances": [
    "vague_claims",
    "excessive_motion",
    "too_many_primary_ctas",
    "burying_pricing"
  ],
  "strategies": [
    {
      "strategy_id": "conversion_with_proof",
      "intent": "convert_visitors_to_trial_or_template_get",
      "hero_focus": "clear_value_prop_plus_primary_cta",
      "information_order": [
        "top_nav",
        "headline",
        "subheadline",
        "primary_cta",
        "builder_credit",
        "hero_visual",
        "workflow_features",
        "customer_logos",
        "product_sections_with_visuals",
        "testimonials_carousel",
        "pricing",
        "faq",
        "final_cta",
        "footer"
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "keep_cta_copy_non_pushy",
        "anchor_claims_with_proof",
        "limit_motion_to_microinteractions",
        "use_scannable_sections"
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "product_clarity_first",
      "intent": "reduce_uncertainty_before_asking_for_action",
      "hero_focus": "what_it_is_who_its_for",
      "information_order": [
        "top_nav",
        "headline",
        "subheadline",
        "hero_visual",
        "workflow_features",
        "product_sections_with_visuals",
        "customer_logos",
        "testimonials",
        "pricing",
        "primary_cta",
        "faq",
        "footer"
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "avoid_cta_overuse",
        "show_interfaces_before_pricing",
        "keep_copy_concrete",
        "ensure_section_order_supports_comprehension"
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "pricing_led_transparency",
      "intent": "win_by_clear_plans_and_upfront_value",
      "hero_focus": "simple_promise_plus_plan_anchor",
      "information_order": [
        "top_nav",
        "headline",
        "subheadline",
        "customer_logos",
        "pricing",
        "plan_benefits",
        "features",
        "testimonials",
        "faq",
        "final_cta",
        "footer"
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "keep_plans_simple",
        "avoid_surprise_constraints",
        "reinforce_value_per_tier",
        "place_proof_near_pricing"
      ]
    }
  ]
}\n\n
<SELECTED_STRATEGY>\n
{
  "strategy_id": "conversion_with_proof",
  "intent": "convert_visitors_to_trial_or_template_get",
  "hero_focus": "clear_value_prop_plus_primary_cta",
  "information_order": [
    "top_nav",
    "headline",
    "subheadline",
    "primary_cta",
    "builder_credit",
    "hero_visual",
    "workflow_features",
    "customer_logos",
    "product_sections_with_visuals",
    "testimonials_carousel",
    "pricing",
    "faq",
    "final_cta",
    "footer"
  ],
  "risk_controls": [
    "keep_cta_copy_non_pushy",
    "anchor_claims_with_proof",
    "limit_motion_to_microinteractions",
    "use_scannable_sections"
  ]
}\n\n
<TASK>\n
Create a multi-page marketing website for a new project planning tool called “Mainline”. The site should include a home/landing page with a clear hero and product screenshots, a trusted-by logo section, feature sections, testimonials, pricing plans, an FAQ section, and a final CTA. Also include separate About, Pricing, FAQ, Contact, and Login pages accessible from the top navigation.
\n\n

You are the Step-5 Executor in a multi-stage AI-CDO system.

Input you receive:
- UI Intent JSON
- Full CDO Brief JSON
- Selected Strategy object

Your output MUST be a JSON manifest only:

{
  "files": [{"path": "...", "content": "..."}],
  "instructions": "npm install && npm run dev"
}

Rules:
- Obey UI Intent strictly
- Follow selected strategy exactly
- Generate modular Next.js App Router code (TypeScript + Tailwind)
- No explanations
- No markdown
- Output valid JSON only

<ROLE=EXECUTOR>
<BUCKET=B>
<UI_INTENT>
{
  "strategy_id": "B1_contextual_cta",
  "primary_focus": "balanced_conversion_across_marketing_routes",
  "cta_policy": {
    "timing": "always_visible_on_primary_hero_and_contextual_in_section_footers",
    "intensity": "medium"
  },
  "proof_policy": "use_existing_copy_and_layout_without_new_claims",
  "content_density": "medium",
  "motion_policy": "subtle_scroll_based_only_using_existing_aos",
  "creative_license": "restricted_to_copy_tweaks_and_layout_within_existing_brand",
  "language_style": "precise_confident_neutral",
  "interaction_restraint": "moderate_no_popups_or_intrusive_modals"
}
<CDO_BRIEF>
{
  "primary_intent": "Guide visitors from informational pages toward sign-up and contact flows in a balanced way while preserving the existing Sustainable Next.js design and performance.",
  "key_risks": [
    "Introducing overly aggressive CTAs that feel out of place with the current brand tone.",
    "Adding heavy or custom animation that harms performance or conflicts with existing AOS usage.",
    "Creating routing inconsistencies or broken links between /signup, /signin, and /contact.",
    "Diluting existing content hierarchy on marketing pages by overloading sections with CTAs.",
    "Accidentally introducing hard-coded external URLs or environment-specific assumptions."
  ],
  "strategic_principles": [
    "Reuse existing components, Tailwind utility classes, and design tokens defined in globals.css.",
    "Keep new CTAs concise and action-oriented, matching the neutral, confident voice of existing copy.",
    "Ensure any new links use Next.js routing primitives and the current app directory structure.",
    "Maintain accessibility by using semantic buttons/links and preserving keyboard focus order.",
    "Avoid new global side effects; changes should be local to relevant components and routes."
  ],
  "avoidances": [
    "Do not introduce new global providers or modify src/app/layout.tsx behaviorally.",
    "Do not add autoplaying media, carousels, or novel animation libraries beyond existing AOS.",
    "Do not make ungrounded promises about sustainability impact or business outcomes.",
    "Do not change authentication logic or NextAuth configuration; only adjust surface UI.",
    "Do not hard-code absolute URLs; rely on relative paths like /signup or /contact."
  ],
  "strategies": [
    {
      "strategy_id": "B1_contextual_cta",
      "intent": "Embed clear but non-intrusive CTAs on key marketing pages to gently direct users toward /signup and /contact from the homepage, About, Pricing, Portfolio, and FAQ routes.",
      "hero_focus": "mid_funnel_education_with_action_paths",
      "information_order": [
        "contextual_value_statement",
        "brief_supporting_detail",
        "primary_cta_to_signup",
        "secondary_cta_to_contact"
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Use existing button styles (e.g. .btn) and typography tokens for visual consistency.",
        "Limit each major section to at most one primary and one secondary CTA.",
        "Keep new copy generic and accurate to the current template (e.g. Get started, Contact us).",
        "Confirm that all new CTAs resolve to existing routes and do not create dead links."
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "B2_auth_entry_clarity",
      "intent": "Clarify when users should sign in versus sign up by adding lightweight guidance and links between /signin and /signup without altering authentication logic.",
      "hero_focus": "friction_reduction_for_returning_vs_new_users",
      "information_order": [
        "page_purpose_label",
        "short_explanation_for_new_vs_existing_users",
        "primary_cta_matching_page_purpose",
        "cross_link_to_alternate_auth_route"
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Keep messages short and neutral, avoiding any mention of pricing or guarantees.",
        "Use existing components such as FailedLogin and UserRegistered only for visual consistency.",
        "Do not modify next-auth or SessionProviderComp behavior.",
        "Verify links between /signin and /signup are correct and accessible."
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "B3_feature_discovery_pathways",
      "intent": "Encourage exploration of the Documentation and Blog pages from the homepage and About page to support users who need more information before converting.",
      "hero_focus": "information_discovery_prior_to_conversion",
      "information_order": [
        "value_of_learning_more",
        "link_to_documentation",
        "link_to_blog",
        "gentle_prompt_toward_signup_after_content_consumption"
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Place discovery links in logical, low-friction positions (e.g. beneath explanatory sections).",
        "Avoid repeating the same link clusters multiple times on a single page.",
        "Respect existing typography hierarchy so that CTAs do not overpower headings.",
        "Ensure navigation patterns remain consistent with Header and DocNavigation components."
      ]
    }
  ]
}
<SELECTED_STRATEGY>
{
  "strategy_id": "B1_contextual_cta",
  "intent": "Embed clear but non-intrusive CTAs on key marketing pages to gently direct users toward /signup and /contact from the homepage, About, Pricing, Portfolio, and FAQ routes.",
  "hero_focus": "mid_funnel_education_with_action_paths",
  "information_order": [
    "contextual_value_statement",
    "brief_supporting_detail",
    "primary_cta_to_signup",
    "secondary_cta_to_contact"
  ],
  "risk_controls": [
    "Use existing button styles (e.g. .btn) and typography tokens for visual consistency.",
    "Limit each major section to at most one primary and one secondary CTA.",
    "Keep new copy generic and accurate to the current template (e.g. Get started, Contact us).",
    "Confirm that all new CTAs resolve to existing routes and do not create dead links."
  ]
}
<TASK>
Implement strategy B1_contextual_cta in the Sustainable Next.js project by updating the main marketing components so that each key page offers a clear but balanced path to /signup and /contact: in src/components/hero_index.tsx, add a primary “Get started” button linking to /signup and a secondary “Contact us” button linking to /contact using the existing .btn styling and Next.js Link, keeping motion subtle by relying only on any existing AOS attributes; in src/components/hero_sub_index.tsx, extend its props and layout so that About (/src/app/(site)/about/page.tsx), Pricing (/src/app/(site)/pricing/page.tsx), Portfolio (/src/app/(site)/portfolio/page.tsx), and FAQ (/src/app/(site)/faqs/page.tsx) can optionally render a compact CTA row beneath the title/description with a primary button to /signup and a text-style secondary link to /contact, and then wire those pages to pass appropriate CTA props; in components that already present conversion-like content such as src/components/preferred_plan_index.tsx and src/components/cardbox.tsx, append a single CTA row at the bottom of the main section that reiterates the “Get started” path to /signup without adding more than one new primary and one new secondary action per page; ensure all new links are relative (e.g. “/signup”, “/contact”), respect existing typography and color tokens from globals.css, avoid adding new animations or providers, and keep the CTA copy concise, neutral, and accurate to a generic SaaS-style sustainable template.
